Here’s the deal with this watch. Buy it. The value for a barley over $100 watch is immense. Compared to other brands like mvmt and Daniel Wellington and other stupid fashion brands claiming to be giving you an amazing deal by cutting out the middle mad on a cheap ass Chinese quartz movement watch(what a joke) If you really think about it, You are getting an aluminum bezel gmt automatic watch with actually pretty amazing finishing with the appropriate short cuts to get you the cost. After reviewing this watch immensely before I bought it I was convinced I would give it 5 stars when it arrived. But alas I cannot. The quality control just isn’t quite there. However, it is clear that they are listening to reviews and complaints and trying to make it better.
Issue #1. If you look at my second picture, it’s difficult to see at a distance but there is a tiny nick in the bezel that it came with. It doesn’t bug me enough to send it back and let’s be honest I will probably put more nicks in it in the future. But I expect a brand new watch to not have that.

Issue #2. Sizing the bracelet is a BITCH. some of the screwed are loosened by just wearing the bracelet. But two of the screws were IMPOSSIBLE to remove. In fact those links barely move because the screw is so tight. I could not remove one of them. And the other one took me an hour and a half to remove. The screwdriv
top_profane.title: Quality control issues. But amazing value.

Update, 2021-04-04. Had it for only 2 weeks and the movement has stopped running.
It is at full charge, and will not wind any tighter. So the one I got, the movement is junk.

Perhaps, if you are a watchmaker, or can clean & repair watches, this might be a nice watch, but at this point, either there is junk inside the case like hair or dust, the movement is garbage, or I got a defective one.
I'll try contacting the seller next, but this is probably a junk movement.

It is still beautiful, but if it won't run at all, that's a 0 rating.

Original review:
So, I was very skeptical of buying this watch. For all the features @ $300 US, it seemed a bit much.

The problem was, the damn thing is SO GORGEOUS.
I HAD to buy it.

TL;DR, if you LIKE it, BUY IT.

Now, I'm going to write a pretty darn good review.
A LONG review.

The crystal *IS* Sapphire, according to a professional testing device a friendly jeweler owns. It is not as cold to the lip-touch as other sapphire crystals I own, but it passes the expensive tool test.

If I remember, I'll post a review a year or a month from now about the durability of the crystal.
I am wearing this gorgeous thing every day now, for at least a month, just to test the crystal, & accuracy.

Presentation: God Damn Perfect. When th
top_profane.title: UPDATE:  2 weeks, doesn't run anymore.  Skeptical, but turned out very happy.

top_profane.text: Despite the pictures, this industrial really looks like crap you'd get at Claire's Boutique. The "gems" are tacky and tend to fall out. Would not recommend. I ended up removing the crappy pink pieces of plastic and gluing in natural amethyst stones. Looks MUCH better! Oh, these are a little hard to insert, as they screw together at the decorative center part. Thus, you have 3 pieces: 2 bar ends and a decorative center. The center doesn't rotate, so screwing in the bars was a bit of a struggle. No infection or other issues, so I assume that the metal is of decent quality.

top_profane.text:
When I ordered these fairly simple, yet boho-inspired yoga pants, I expected their arrival to be as straight-forward as they would be if offered from a catalog. This was not the case. They took a month and a half to arrive. Turns out, it was cause they came from China. I'm learning my lesson as of late that pretty much anything greatly discounted on Amazon that doesn't have a major brand attached to it, hails from some black hole in Asia, where they make....everything. My advise? Don't buy clothing on here. UNLESS it's Prime. Even then, if the photos depict Asian mannequins, and you're grass-fed Milwaukeean, the sizing isn't gonna work. And if it's not Prime, the returns will be a drawn-out bitch. There are COUNTLESS US stores that carry everything you see on Amazon, you just gotta watch for sales. I think we've mostly all learned that just about everything on here that seems to good to be true money-wise, IS. You want boho and can't afford Anthropologie? Check out SEARS. Yup, Sears. Their Junior section has been carrying *great* boho for decades now. They have massive sales, constant coupons and like all American stores, you'll never wait more than a week. You want REALLY cool, original, lush boho? Check out ETSY. Not to mention, there's so much genuine vintage on there that you won't have to stress over not being able to go thrifting right now.

Having said this, a
top_profane.title: Amazon is to no-name clothing as oil is to___.

This hat was purchased for my daughter, because she looks amazing wearing a hat. It is listed as a hat for those who have hair loss, but she does not have that issue. The thing is, this hat is totally stying! I love the way it fits her, and it is wonderful for many reasons. The grosgrain ribbon can be utilized to tuck in accessories to match an outfit, a flower, a feather, a pin, and so on. Since she is a professional entertainer as well as an artist, she can use this hat to dress up an outfit that is used on stage. It is extremely well made. I think that if it were listed differently as something that is universal it would be more commonly worn, since it is adorable. No lie. Really cute!

I received this product on a promotional discount from the seller for exchange of my honest unbiased review. These comments and my evaluation here are my own opinions. I am not receiving any other monetary compensation and I am not required to give a good review. I am merely asked to give my own fair appraisal of my feelings about each item, or how it seems to fit the purpose it was purchased for. I am not associated with the seller in any way. top_rant.text:
Since I purchased my Apple Watch I've been using the black stock rubber watch band that came with it almost exclusively. I did try the Apple Watch Band, Penom Fully Magnetic Closure Clasp Mesh Loop Milanese Stainless Steel Bracelet Strap for Apple iWatch Sport & Edition 42mm - Black for about a week but ended up returning it because the magnet was not strong enough to hold the position on the strap, causing me to constantly have to readjust it. I loved the way that watch band looked but couldn't deal with adjusting it all day long.

Since then I've continued to use the boring yet reliable stock rubber watch band until I came across this leather replacement. I've been using it now for almost 2 weeks and am very happy with it.

Some users have reported that it feels uncomfortable and frays quickly. I have not experienced either of these problems, so I'm not sure if they received a defective item or are doing something different. My watch band still looks and feel perfect!

I am very pleased with the magnetic strap on this watch band - it has multiple magnets placed along the end of it that do a great job securing the position you close it at. As I noted with the previous band I tried this was the major issue I had with that band.

I do think this band looks very nice with my Apple Sport Watch as well. I've always liked leather watch ban
top_rant.title: I loved the way that watch band looked but couldn't deal ...

top_rant.text:
>>> UPDATED REVIEW <<<
As promised, I am updating my review since the seller BOCK updated this Amazon ad's photos and descriptions. Except BOCK totally moved the DOUBLE winder to a separate ad and made this ad just the SINGLE version. So let's get on with the review:

> The product arrived in 2 days as promised. The winder's packaging box was packed with air bubble wrap and inside the sealed box around the watch winder itself were layers of styrofoam! The watch winder was inside a plastic bag that was sealed. And the see-thru plastic part of the winder was covered by a sticky plastic protector. When I peeled it off, the see-thru plastic was pristine!
> The winder box's faux leather has a nice matte type finish and the white threaded stitching was flawless. So I liked the way it looks!
> The Motor Noise... WOW! They weren't kidding when they said their Mabuchi (I have no idea what that is) motor was quiet. There's a slight and I mean slight buzz of a motor when it's turning, but as soon as you shut the lid, it's silent! Big thumbs up there.
> The hinge seemed solidly built and the lid closes and opens easily. When the lid closes, it makes a nice solid and tight seal.
> The rotating watch holder also appears solid and rotates smoothly and as I mentioned quietly.
> Despite the ad, the Single winder (at least the one I rec
top_rant.title: Great winder & can use batteries

top_rant.text: There's only one word to describe a band like Gojira and that one word is incredible. These guys are certainly without a doubt one of the most creative, unique and original bands in the metal genre today. Their 2008 release, "The Way of All Flesh" is an amazingly stunning and powerful work of pure, hypnotic grooving progressive/death metal that is dare I say just as powerful as the band's name itself. The first thing that I must have to say is that the music on this album here is just incredibly breathtaking and extremely heavy hitting throughout. The songwriting and musicianship is just supremely tight here with the guitars, bass and drums all playing in perfectly intricate formation throughout. Each track here is just chock filled with crushingly heavy and powerful riffs, plodding bass lines, amazingly balanced drumming, and intensely powerful vocals throughout. The production value here on TWOAF is also very outstanding and crystal clear as well, and also another thing that I would like to point out on what also makes Gojira incredible are the lyrics that they write which are very poetic and thought provoking, dealing with topics such as nature, environmental issues, spirituality, life and death, the redeeming possibility of pain and suffering, and the necessity of introspection. The vocals of Joe Duplantier are just intense, powerful and harsh, yet very clear simult
top_rant.title: An album so stunningly powerful beyond expectations!!! \m/ \m/

top_rant.text:
Heads-up: This is a long review and includes suggestions for improvements that I personally would make as well as improvements or modifications that I feel are necessary to avoid having an annoying costume. Overall this is a pretty great costume and really only NEEDS a couple of modifications (tack sew the collar so it isn't floppy, make padded arm bands so the sleeves actually poof out, and use a different petticoat). Read on for a LOT more detail. (and check back for an updated review including before and after pics once I have made the modifications I feel are necessary)

As an added note for my kid friendly Disney cosplayers: THE PROVIDED HOOP SKIRT IS VERY MUCH NOT KID FRIENDLY. If you are the type to sit on the floor and hug kids and let them sit with you, the hoop skirt will do nothing but cause problems and stand a chance at damaging your costume. I'm actually not sure why a simple fluffy petticoat wasn't provided, rather than a bulky hoop skirt. Skip the hoop skirt and buy a soft petticoat (or skip the underskirt all together if that's your style).

I am a competitive cosplayer, but have resolved to purchase cosplays to wear if I don't intend on competing in them, rather than spend time and money (and stress) making them myself. This is the first cosplay I have ever purchased online and I have to say that I am very pleasantly surprised! Of course, t
top_rant.title: Pleasantly Surprised, needs minor modifications, 5'3" 140lbs, ordered XL, fits slightly loose

top_rant.text:
Music and Beanies often do not work well together, either you need a beanie to keep warm, making no room for your headphones or you just cannot live without your music and would rather suffer through the blistering cold on those winter nights. With audio enthusists such as myself, we always seem to choose the latter suffering the flu before we would give up listening to our music. SoundBot aims to help us audiophiles with this small, simple, yet intuitive design that is sure to be on any music lover’s Christmas list.

When I first received the SoundBot SB210 Music Beanie I already knew I was going to love it. My real-world job entails me staying outside in the cold for long periods of time, leaving me either very cold or music-less. Often times, I dream of having a way to enjoy both of these things at the same time. I have had music beanies in the past, and they are either very uncomfortable on the sides or heavy and just not easily maintained. Battery life is also something I would consider below par on many Beanie headphones in the past I have used. I decided SoundBot’s iteration of these headphones just couldn’t be as bad as the experiences I have had in the past and I was actually very excited to give them a shot, not only was I surprised when I put them on, but shocked.

When the package first arrived along with a few other products to review I was surp
top_rant.title: An Awesome Beanie and a Great Christmas Present for Just About Anybody

top_rant.text:
For the price and the apparent reputation of the brand, this should be a tremendously good product. Unfortunately, tremendous failure is all that I've encountered. Read on to find out the lowdown...

«««««««««««»»»»»»»»»»»
The Praise (Good Things)
«««««««««««»»»»»»»»»»»
+ Good-looking frames in a selection of colors that can be mixed and matched
+ Interchangeable lenses in a huge selection for different activities and appearances
+ Extra lens features such as polarized, anti-reflective back coating, and many others
+ Lifetime "warranty" exchange options for lenses

««««««««««»»»»»»»»»»
The Raze (Bad Things)
««««««««««»»»»»»»»»»
- Temple screws loosen quickly under normal use and must be re-tightened frequently
- Despite all claims of high quality, the lenses are anything but in actual use - to the point of nearly complete uselessness
- Lenses stood up to about two weeks of light use before exhibiting problems and damage and in some cases exhibited problems while stored
- The warranting of the lenses requires a "nominal fee" for replacement - of over 25% of the cost of new lenses

«««««««««««««»»»»»»»»»»»»»
The Haze (Things to Consider)
«««««««««««««»»»»»»»»»»»»»
* With the broad spectrum of lenses, knowing which lenses will tint things is not always
top_rant.title: A collossal waste of money for easy scratches and cracks, loose screws, and an exhorbitant "guarantee" fee

top_rant.text:
I'm 5'2"
155 pounds
I carry my weight in my thighs and rear.
 
My measurements are as follows:
Ankle 9"
Calf 16" 
Mid thigh 23"
Upper thigh 28"
Hips 43”
Waist 30”
 

The “Extra Firm Footless Graduated Compression Microfiber Leggings Opaque Pants (20-30 mmHg) with Control Top (Medium) “
Brand: Terramed Just Think Comfort

Size medium is what fits me.

The large did not give adequate compression, and fit more like a tight pair of yoga pants.

These are thicker, warmer, and only slightly see-through on my thighs if i bend over or squat. 

It has built-in undies to keep your nether region opaque. 

Of course, since I have a booty, when i bend over, you can see the built-in underwear if there’s decent lighting. 

I didn’t have this problem with the large, but the large is useless to me because it doesn’t give me the compression i need. 
 
I have POTS and was recently diagnosed w/ EDS and Fibromylagia.

I was told to get high-waisted compression stockings to help with symptoms. 

I’m exhausted from my physical symptoms and from trying on
compression. 

So here’s 1 review on multiple items. 

Hopefully this will help anyone with a similar body type to mine. 
 
The sizing for compression
top_rant.title: Comfortable and warmer than other leggings

top_rant.text:
*** I am an Amazon Vine member. As such, I have received this product free of charge for review purposes.***

Advertised Product Performance: These crease protectors are advertised as being able to prevent creases in your shoes, keep your shoes looking brand new, improve the appearance of old shoes, and to extend the lifespan of old shoes.
They are also advertised as being able to keep your favorite shoes dry every day, enhance the comfort of your big and little toes, and be easily trimmed to size.
They are advertised for Men’s shoe sizes 9-15 and as being compatible with Air Force’s, Jordan’s, Dunk’s, Yeezy’s, Kobe’s, Lebron’s, Smith’s, Timberland’s, CAT’s, and all other sneakers (as per the image in the product description).

First Thoughts: There is zero part of me that thinks these crease protectors will work as advertised. If the description is to be believed, I am sure, with 100% positive certainty, that these are a waste of money.

Let’s break it down, shall we?

• These crease protectors are advertised as being able to prevent creases in your shoes - The only way they would prevent creases is if they didn’t allow the shoe to bend, which would require a form-fitted, hardened material with no flexibility. They are, however, made from a soft, bendy, plastic material.

• keep your shoes looking brand new – An unrea
top_rant.title: Minimal Visual Difference - Not Useable When Actually Wearing Shoes

top_rant.text:
This dress is indeed  worthy of a detailed if not plaintively emotional review. The reality is, if I wanted to wear a scratchy burlap sack, I could have gone to any of my local feed stores. After all, I do live in Oklahoma; and there is no lack of businesses providing  necessary goods for farmers and ranchers.
I see there are very positive reviews on this garment. I feel that speaks to the indomitable human spirit and the subjectivity of empirical experience. I'm sure that nearly everyone is able to have a distinct experience with this particular dress. However, in my case, it felt like the kind of polyester that you might have seen your third grade teacher wearing in perhaps the mid-70's. And of course I just dated myself there.
The texture reminded me of the extremely uncomfortable-looking dresses or slacks which harken back to be restrictive and misogynistic corsets of old. The rare original edition by Nathaniel Hawthorne, that moral narrative of the life of Hester Prynne, recounts that she was offered this dress in lieu of The Scarlet Letter to be emblazoned on her forehead; to which she curtly replied: "I'll take the letter thank you very much," while fishing a blazing A-shaped iron from the smoldering coals.
But this dress, right out of the bag, felt less like a garment and more like the kind of torture apparel commonly used by  nuns or priests in the 160
top_rant.title: It serves a unique purpose

top_rant.text:
Automatic mechanical watches have never been my cup of tea, but this one kept popping up in online ads and catching my attention. At $100 for a Chinese fashion watch, my interest stopped at window shopping, but when I saw it on Amazon for a lot less than that, and then realized I could shave a few more bucks off the price with some reward points, I decided to give it a try.

The watch comes securely packed in a neat, black Lige box with a fold-over, magnetic lid, and small instruction booklet, cleaning cloth, warranty card, and a little tool you can use for pushing the two hidden buttons on opposite sides of the crown.

Features and Functions ...

The watch is equipped with hour/minute hands only (no second hand), and 3 sub-dials: day-of-week; calendar day, and 24-hour dial with underlying sun/moon/a.m./p.m. disc. The day-of-week and calendar day dials are adjusted by two hidden pushbuttons on opposite sides of the crown. One push = one unit on the corresponding dial. In normal operation, or when you’re adjusting the watch for the first time, don’t get thrown if both sub-dials don’t advance at midnight. The calendar-day sub-dial advances at midnight. The day-of-week subdial advances between 1:30 and 2:30 a.m.

The 24-hour dial revolves once every 24 hours. The sun/moon disc beneath the dial is NOT a moon phase indicator ... just a
top_rant.title: Great looking "bracelet" ... but look elsewhere if you want an actual watch.

top_rant.text:
Well now!!! This one's something special, isn't it? A little nicer than the rest.
Of all the Casio's I own, this PRW-3500 is my favorite.
NOTE: This watch's LCD display is MUCH clearer and brighter than the Amazon image(s) show! See other web images, it's very impressive.
For comparison, I currently own:

PRG-270-1 (black, some orange print)
PRG-270D-7CR (titanium band)
PRW-3000-2BCR (blue/purple)
GW-9400-1CR Rangeman
WV-300 Wave Ceptor (discontinued)
And now, the PRW-3500-1CR

All of the new Casio sensor v3.0 watches have come a long way in accuracy. Where I couldn't accurately calibrate the earlier Pathfinders, I can now calibrate everything and get very accurate readings with the new Pro Trek series. Here are some comments on the functions:

COMPASS: Very accurate, works as good as my mechanical compasses. The movable bezel on this model is redundant, but still very useful and looks classy. Note: all magnetic compasses are negatively affected by magnetism, metal objects, etc. Best not to be used in the car, near electrical equipment or audio speakers which have powerful magnets. Best out in the field - and always calibrate right before using.

BAROMETER: Accurate to within .05 inHg. More accurate than than my iPhone Weather Channel App, because it's real-time, whereas with the phone app you have
top_rant.title: This One's Special!

top_rant.text:
(SCROLL DOWN FOR PROS AND CONS LIST. First paragraphs are my impression and description.)

I really love this set of nose rings. I usually wear stud style nose rings but every now and then i like to change it up. Hoop nose rings, in my opinion, can really change up your look and style and can noticeably add to an outfit. They are a more noticeable, eye catching style, which will be noticed easier than small studs that tend to be more subtle. The open hoop style is one I prefer and I find them easier to wear. It leaves some open space in your nostril that I find far more comfortable for day to day wear. (TMI warning! LoL – They are WAY easier to deal with when you need to blow your nose! When I have a full hoop in my nose it can really hurt trying to blow my nose and having to maneuver it.)

This set of nose rings came in a handy little plastic box that can hold them safely for travel or storage. They were each individually wrapped in plastic sleeves before I opened them. There are five colors provided: silver, yellow/gold, black, a blue chrome finish, and a multi-colored chrome finish that reflects different colors in different light (it leans predominantly more green/yellow and resembles an ‘oil slick’ appearance). The color selection includes all the ‘basic’ colors for a wardrobe I could want. They are core colors that will match most of my jewelry and oth
top_rant.title: Must be put in from the INSIDE of your nostril going OUT for the gap to be hidden!

top_rant.text:
These are good pearl earrings for the price. The pearls are the size promised. They aren't quite perfectly round, they don't quite match, and they have a bit less luster than pricier pearls would. That said, the posts are hypoallergenic (I am very allergic to pot metals), and I can relax wearing them knowing that if I loose an earring, I haven't lost the money. This brand has all different sizes of pearls with the sterling silver coated in rhodium posts at reasonable price points. Given the price of pearls, this can be a good way to try some different sizes out, before getting fancier pearls in a gold setting.

Are they real?: First off, Jaqueline Kennedy's famous pearls were fake. Good quality fakes are pricier than lower grades of pearls. If you don't believe me, then look for a gem show near you, pay the entry fee, and see what you can get for what price with pearls by the strand. You will be surprised. Meanwhile, I have some very gorgeous 1950s vintage fake pearls that I got new old stock that are much nicer than any real pearls in my price range. There's a huge difference between a high quality fake and some garbage plastic fake. Jaqueline Kennedy's fake pearls were valued at $400-$500 by Sotherby's in the mid-1990s, to give an idea of how good fakes cost more than lower end real pearls. Now that that is out of the way, these Abbie+Cleo pearls are re
top_rant.title: Hypoallergenic posts, for real; pearls are real and are good enough to look fine

top_exclaim.text:
I REALLY WANTED TO LOVE THIS, BUT....so much is disappointing. First of all the fit. I am a very tiny lady; 5' 2" and weigh 90 lbs. I knew this was 100 % cotton and I wash my clothes so I knew it would shrink. I ordered a medium to be sure it was roomy enough after shrinkage. I tried it on right out of the bag and could barely get my head through the neck opening, which only has a single " button" at the neck. The other buttons on the front placket are fake- don't open or close, so just decoration! It was very long, which I anticipated and knew I would have to take up. Since I normally wear a size 2, this should have been more than roomy enough for me to get into!!! NOT!
I washed it as I always do and the first major issue was a good 10 inches of the beautiful decorative metalic thread on the hem disintigrated and unravelled!!! Destroyed! A very noticable gap!!!
It is a very good thing that I am a professional seamstress and can sew anything! I knew before I washed it that I would have to take apart the front placket and make all the buttons operable and that would take a few hours to re-make the placket, which is only decorative and not fully made! Since I knew too, that I would have to shorten it, I knew I would have that left-over fabric to create the placket!
I will take the length up by taking apart the seam that connects the skirt part to the bodice,
top_exclaim.title: I REALLY WANTED TO LOVE THIS SO MUCH, BUT........

top_exclaim.text:

This band matched my shirt so I want to work in it and I swear I'm updating this status because I don't know what to do anymore. When it 1st happened I could have cried, if it wasn't for the fact that I'm a grown man and my dad taught me not to; I would have.So today while getting off the highwayTo Lakewood where I live!!!! take Sharp turn!!!! because that's what this exit calls for!!! The magnet's Failed!!!! on this watch band and my watch fell out the window and people kept running it! over!!! I IMMEDIATELY!! U-Turned illegally to try to save MY BABY!!! BUT!!! people kept RUNNING IT OVER!!!! before I could do anything The watch Was completely destroyed like completely!!!!! I've never been so sad in my life because of This band. I almost died trying to get my watch off the ground of the highway exit..but it was dead..I never even let my kids touch it, this watch band is the DEVIL!!!!
I'm calling Amazon Customer service, this is BS

top_exclaim.text: I am very disappointed and dissatisfied with this band!  I was getting more steps than usual, so I checked my Fitbit app... I was sitting down and just shaking my wrist, and the app was adding steps with me just sitting!!!  I wanted to put my old original band back on but the little pegs that you can remove the band with we’re gone!!!!  I tried taking it off and it Won’t come off!  During trying to take it off, my Fitbit case got gadges in it!!! I want my old band on it so I can wear it!!! I’m literally crying!!!!  I’m not rich!!!  Please REPLACE my Fitbit Versa!  If it wasn’t for this band I bought, it would be in new shape!!! Now it has sharp gadges!!!  I can’t wear it , it will cut me!!  Please replace it!  Rose gold one!  Thank you!  Ann Chagnon. top_five_star_obscene.text: There are a lot of black PVD SS mesh bands available in all price ranges, but this one is very high quality!  No sharp edges, black doesn't chip off like the cheaper crap, the clasp is excellent and it's just a much nicer design.  YOU GET WHAT YOU PAY FOR IN LIFE, so if you've got a nice watch, and you're looking to put this style of mesh band on it, don't be buying a $25 piece of crap and expecting it to be good to go.  This IS the one, it's worth the money!
top_five_star_obscene.title: YOU GET WHAT YOU PAY FOR!

I have to be honest  I love these sunglasses but I don't appreciate them break so easily, I did not break them lets get that straight, like I mentioned the flowers were screwed on & I think maybe if after they were each screwed in they could have a touch of glue to provide a little more protection. The screws needed to be screwed in as tight as they could be yet they weren't or they just came I loose that easily. top_five_star_obscene.text: Love Wiley X above all others. Now these are high quality, very comfortable, fit to your face like they were custom molded to you specifically and they don't fatigue you (headaches/tension stress). Came with a sports lanyard, both removable and permanent side guards if you use for work safety glasses and the fancy bags to protect everything in the fancy case. The only thing is that this series model is NOT MADE IN THE USA. I researched and reached out to Wiley X and these are authentic , but they do have a few models produced in Tiawan due to demand needs and shit. They have a list of the models made in the US and Tiawan. Basically the tactical/black ops series and a few others are US made. The sporty ones like these are contracted out. Again they are still high quality to be expected and they ARE NOT CHINESE pieces of shit like everything else..... So I suggest you get em'.. These are bitchn shades for sure!
top_five_star_obscene.title: High Quality Wiley X (Disclaimer)

top_five_star_obscene.text: I was fine with my 10+ year old underwear from a foreign company that I loved, but the wife hated.  There was nothing wrong with them except that they were more than a decade old.  So I ordered over $160 dollars worth of Calvin Kleins and as soon as they came, I threw them in the washer and chucked my decade old inventory away.  BIG MISTAKE because those CK ones (that you get on Amazon) were the worst pieces of crap ever!  Don't get CK on Amazon, period.  So what did I do?  I washed the only pair I wore, complained to Amazon and sent those pieces of garbage back for a full refund.  I was always a HANES guy in my 20s, so I thought what the hell, and just got 21 pairs and went commando for over a week before they arrived.  I had one underwear that I had to reuse and wash every other night like some villager in a fishing village until these came.  But when they did, Hallelujah! They were a perfect, snug fit, and I felt young again! Life was fantastic, all thanks to HANES.  Thank you.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Commando NO MORE!

top_five_star_obscene.text: These are boss. Looking at the world through rose colored glasses now. For the $2.50 I paid, they're also pretty damn sturdy. I figured they'd be flimsy as hell but they're better than the crap from the gas station.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Love 'em.

top_five_star_obscene.text:
[Note: this is for the blue and gold colorway]. These are a pretty convincing copy of Dior's Technologic sunnies. When it comes to ordering sunglasses online it is a crap shoot every time even when specks are included and in this case, they thankfully turned out to be the perfect, slightly over sized aviator with beautiful deep blue lenses I was looking for. This particular color seems to be a big trend this year and its great to buy these instead of spending on a trend that may only last a few minutes.

It does seem that based on what color you purchase, the sizing might be different. On some people these can look huge and on others normal.Mine in this blue color are about 6 inches at the widest top crossbar area. The lenses themselves are about 2 and a quarter long. I did notice that men seem to prefer this style over the So Real frames also by Dior as these come out wider at the temples. These glasses have a nice weight to them but the arms feel very loosely screwed. It concerns me they might break/fall off. These also came with a black dust bag. Overall mine came exactly as pictured and are holding up well with a quality slightly better than F21 or H and M.

FTC This product was provided for free or at a discount by the manufacturer in exchange for an honest and unbiased review.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Nice dupe of the Technologic frames, nice change from So Real's

top_five_star_obscene.text:
I love this hoodie! I was worried it would be that crappy cheap, thin, slick material you get with some of these cheaper printed hoodies, but it's actually very nice material, and quite a bit warmer than I expected it to be. The fleece inside is soft, and it's super cozy. The print isn't super high quality, but it still looks great. Overall it feels very well made with rather sturdy stitching, and is honestly a steal for the price. I ordered another similarly priced hoodie along with this one that looked to be a similar material, and that one turned out to be complete garbage.

This hoodie also runs quite small. I'm normally a solid small in most clothes, but I sized up to L/XL and it fits me perfectly. Readings reviews to see how things fit (especially if they're sized according to normal Asian sizes!) is important.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Thin, but surprisingly warm and comfy, and runs SMALL. If you normally wear small, order L/XL

top_five_star_obscene.text:

I with the material was a little more substantial for duality but I also like not having swamp balls from wrapping them in a thick blanket of cloth on hot days.

Also no scratchy tags! These don't last forever, but then nothing does, and least they are price where I can affort to replace all my pairs annually.
